name: Manila (Philippines)

colors.symbolism: the field, evoking Manila Bay and the sea on which the city was founded
the gold of the city seal — wealth, prestige, and the historic 'Pearl of the Orient' epithet

description: A blue field charged at the centre with the gold seal of the City of Manila, depicting a shield with a sea-lion above sea-waves and surrounded by the city's Latin honorific inscription.
history: Manila, the capital of the Philippines, has used variants of its civic seal — granted by King Philip II of Spain in 1596 — as its primary emblem for centuries. The current municipal flag features the seal on a blue field; the design has been used in the modern era since the city's reorganisation in 1976.

features.symbolism: a blue field as background for the city's historic seal

features.role: Seal of the City of Manila
features.symbolism: The city seal — bearing a shield with a sea-lion (representing Spanish colonial heritage) above stylised waves of Manila Bay, surrounded by the Latin inscription Insigne y Siempre Leal Ciudad de Manila ('Distinguished and Ever Loyal City of Manila'), a title granted by the Spanish Crown in 1574.
